evals.name: list-and-cat-with-connection-check
evals.prompt: I just set up Draft on my machine. Can you list all my pages and show me the contents of the most recent one in markdown format?
evals.expected_output: Agent starts with draft status --json, restores readiness with draft start-server or draft daemon if needed, confirms READY via draft status --json, then uses draft ls --json and draft cat <id> to return the page in markdown for review.
expectations: Agent starts by running draft status --json
Agent treats draft status --json as the authoritative readiness check
Agent runs draft start-server only when status indicates the daemon is offline
Agent runs draft daemon when status indicates the browser is not connected
Agent lists pages with draft ls --json
Agent reads a specific page with draft cat <id> for markdown review
Agent verifies READY before reading content

evals.name: diagnose-browser-missing-before-retry
evals.prompt: My Draft command failed because the browser session seems missing. Diagnose the problem before retrying and tell me the right next command. Do not just retry the original command blindly.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs draft status --json first, identifies the explicit state, and recommends draft daemon only when the daemon is running but the browser is not connected.
expectations: Agent starts by running draft status --json
Agent treats draft status as the authoritative diagnosis step
Agent does not retry the failed live command before checking status
Agent uses draft daemon when the status indicates browser-not-connected

evals.name: create-and-append-with-connection-check
evals.prompt: Create a new Draft page called 'Sprint 42 Retro' and add three bullet points: what went well, what didn't, and action items. Make sure you check if you are connected first.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs draft status --json first, restores readiness if needed, creates the page with draft create --json, captures the page ID from JSON, then appends markdown bullets with draft append --json via stdin.
expectations: Agent starts by running draft status --json
Agent verifies readiness before mutating
Agent creates page with draft create 'Sprint 42 Retro' --json
Agent appends content using draft append <id> --json via stdin
Content includes three bullet points

evals.name: replace-section-on-recent-page
evals.prompt: In my Draft page titled 'Eval Replace Status Seed', replace the Status section with 'Status: Complete - shipped to staging on March 12th'. Check connection first and make sure you're on staging (https://markdown-editor-staging.web.app/).
evals.expected_output: Agent verifies readiness with draft status --json, rejects a READY session on the wrong origin when staging is required, reconnects with the explicit staging URL if needed, finds the correct page with draft ls --json, then runs draft replace <id> --heading 'Status' --json.
expectations: Agent starts by running draft status --json
Agent rejects a READY session on the wrong origin when the user explicitly asks for staging
Agent uses https://markdown-editor-staging.web.app/ when reconnecting to Draft
Agent lists pages with draft ls --json to find the ID for 'Eval Replace Status Seed'
Agent uses draft replace command with --json on the correct ID

evals.name: create-and-publish-roadmap
evals.prompt: I want to share my project roadmap. Create a new Draft page titled 'Project Roadmap', and then publish it to the web. Remember to check your connection first.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs draft status --json, restores readiness if needed, creates 'Project Roadmap' with --json, captures the generated ID, and uses the legacy GLOBAL_PUBLISH_PASSWORD flow to publish with --json.
expectations: Agent starts by running draft status --json
Agent verifies readiness before create and publish
Agent creates the page and captures the ID from JSON output
Agent uses the GLOBAL_PUBLISH_PASSWORD=innosage draft publish <id> --json command with the correct ID
